# Chapter 16 — Service and Repository Implementation Using AI

**Blueprint step:** Steps 13–14 — Define service architecture and repositories

## Context

Chapter 16 turns the Architecture Communication Canvas and EventStorming Detail into running, documented software.
The AI generates service and repository scaffolding from the Canvas and OpenAPI specification; domain logic stays in human hands.

**SharingService** was generated by giving the AI the Architecture Communication Canvas for the Sharing bounded context and the OpenAPI spec from Chapter 14.
The AI produced the service skeleton; the team added the domain invariant enforcement and the event publication logic.

**RecipeRepository** was generated by giving the AI the Recipe Aggregate and the decision from ADR 002 (Event Sourcing for Sharing).
The team reviewed the interface for consistency with the domain model and added the invariant enforcement at the persistence boundary.

## Samples

```
src/main/java/com/cookwithus/
├── sharing/
│   └── SharingService.java        — Application service for recipe publication and revision
└── infrastructure/
    └── RecipeRepository.java      — Repository interface for Recipe persistence
```

## AI Prompt Used

> You are a DDD expert.
> I will provide the Architecture Communication Canvas and the OpenAPI specification for the Sharing bounded context of CookWithUs.
> Generate a Java application service class (`SharingService`) that implements the `publishRecipe` and `reviseRecipe` use cases.
> Use the domain model from Chapter 13.
> The service must enforce all domain invariants before delegating to the repository.
> Add Javadoc to every public method.
> Do not add infrastructure concerns (database, HTTP) to the domain or service classes.
